title: tragacantha coulteri
intro: Tragacantha coulteri, also known as Coulter's needlegrass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function and wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
